title: About Me
text: I am Heba Labib; currently a Physics PhD student at North Carolina State University. I work at the intersection of theoretical condensed matter physics and quantum computing. My research focuses on quantum simulations of many-body systems as well as developing hardware-efficient quantum algorithms. I also work on developing theoretical frameworks for photo-emission spectroscopy and its applications in understanding the electronic properties of materials. I learn best by writing my own notes and explaining things to myself in writing; and thus, the reason I start this journal. In this blog, I will share my research progress, insights, and thoughts on various topics in physics and quantum computing.
